direction: L0 natural language YAML with Two-Phase compiler. YAML files use pure natural language descriptions.
result: 3/3 compile OK after YAML fix pass

direction: Precision-enhanced YAML with structured field-level hints + dependency chain validation.
compiler_strategy: Two-Phase with dependency chain compilation test
result: 3/3 COMPILE_OK + ELAB_OK + SIM_OK. mkCountCF rule conflict fixed (10001 cycles simulation, 0 assertions failed). Full 22-module chain validated.

direction: Multi-agent verification pipeline with automated round-trip compilation. Compiler ARCHITECTURE change.
compiler_strategy: Multi-Agent Pipeline: YAML load -> validate -> prompt assembly -> BSC compile -> status report
result: T01-T03 verified (compile+elab OK). 20 YAML files created by parallel agents. Main finding: agent-created YAML has structural issues (knowledge nesting, dict hints, quoting).

direction: Schema-Enforced YAML Generation with Self-Correcting Format Validator. Focus on INPUT QUALITY.
compiler_strategy: YAML Fixer (auto-fix structure) -> Compiler Pipeline -> BSV Generation
result: 6/6 YAML validated after auto-fixer. JSON Schema + structural fixer eliminates all known YAML format issues.

direction: Top-Down Type Graph Extraction. Build complete type dependency graph first, then use it to provide full type context to LLM when generating each module.
compiler_strategy: Type Graph Extraction -> Topological Sort -> Type-Aware Generation
result: 22 modules analyzed. Computed topological compilation order. Key insight: COMPLETENESS GAP — Headers has 99 typedefs vs our generated subset, DataTypes has 80 typedefs + 16 enums + 27 structs.

direction: Hybrid Deterministic+LLM Architecture: Split the problem — deterministic extraction for simple types (typedefs/enums/structs) + LLM generation for complex behavior (modules/rules/FSMs). Addresses the completeness gap discovered in iter_005.
compiler_strategy: Deterministic Type Extractor + LLM Behavioral Generator -> Merge -> Compile
result: Design documented. 6 fundamentally different directions explored. Next step: implement the hybrid compiler.

direction: Self-Healing Compilation with Error-Guided YAML Refinement. Closed-loop system where BSC compilation errors are automatically parsed, classified, and used to update YAML hints before regeneration. Unlike all prior open-loop approaches (generate once, observe), this implements automated error recovery with up to 3 heal attempts per module.
compiler_strategy: Self-Healing Compiler: YAML load -> Prompt assembly -> Agent generation -> BSC compile -> Error classification -> YAML auto-fix -> Regenerate (loop up to 3x). Error categories: UNDEFINED_TYPE, MISSING_IMPORT, METHOD_NOT_FOUND, RULE_CONFLICT, PROVISO_FAILURE, DERIVING_FAILURE, SYNTAX_ERROR, etc.
result: Self-healing loop demonstrated with PrimUtils (mkCReg type error → YAML fix → regenerate → compile OK). 4/6 compile (67%). Key finding: agent-based YAML regeneration is imprecise for simple syntax fixes, causing error whack-a-mole.

direction: Multi-Version Voting Generation with Compilation Oracle. Instead of generating ONE version per module, generate N=3 versions in parallel. Compile all, select first that works. Leverages LLM non-determinism: P(success) = 1-(1-p)^N. Each version generated independently with different structural approach.
compiler_strategy: For each module: load YAML → generate N prompts with version-specific instructions → spawn N parallel coding agents → compile all N versions → select first that compiles. No healing loop needed — voting handles errors.
result: Settings: 3/3 compile, Headers: 3/3 compile. 6/6 versions across 2 modules (100% success). Multi-version voting eliminates error whack-a-mole from iter_007. Key insight: LLM non-determinism is a feature, not a bug — parallel versions + compiler oracle = robust generation.

direction: Two-Stage Interface-First Generation. Split generation into Stage 1 (interface definitions + type signatures only) and Stage 2 (module bodies implementing verified interfaces). Stage 1 output is verified by compilation before Stage 2 begins, providing a guaranteed-correct contract.
compiler_strategy: Stage 1: Generate interface skeleton (typedefs, enums, structs, interface types, function signatures) → BSC verify → Stage 2: Generate module implementations using verified contract. Each stage has focused prompts with different requirements.
result: Started. PrimUtils Stage 1 generated (3884 bytes) but has expression block error. Two-stage approach shows promise but needs refined Stage 1 prompt (agent included partial function bodies instead of signatures only).

direction: Deterministic Type Extraction + LLM Behavioral Generation (Implementing iter_006 Hybrid). Types extracted from original BSV via regex-based parser — guaranteed correct. LLM scope reduced to behavioral logic only.
compiler_strategy: Type Extractor (deterministic) → Type Skeleton (verified) → LLM Prompt (behavior only) → Merge → Compile
result: 4/6 skeletons compile (Settings, Headers, PrimUtils, SpecialFIFOF). 2/6 fail due to dependency chain gaps (need Utils, DataTypes, Controller — not in test set). 100% type correctness for compiling modules — zero type/syntax errors. Proves hybrid approach eliminates LLM BSV domain knowledge ceiling for type definitions.
